Facts.text: Traditional date for the founding of Japan by Emperor Jimmu
Siricius, bishop of Tarragona, elected as Bishop of Rome; first to style himself Pope
Gregory III begins his reign as Catholic Pope
St Paschal I ends his reign as Pope
Battle at Wayna Daga: Ethiopian and Portuguese troops beat Muslim army
The assault on Copenhagen by Swedish forces is beaten back with heavy losses
Sweden and Prussia sign the Second Treaty of Stockholm
Stamp Act declared unconstitutional in Virginia
Society of Friends petitions Congress for abolition of slavery
Prussian troops occupy Venlo, Netherlands
First session of US Senate open to the public
Anthracite coal is first burned as fuel experimentally in Wilkes-Barre, Pennsylvania
President Madison prohibits trade with Britain for 3rd time in 4 years
Massachusetts Governor Elbridge Gerry signs a redistricting bill - first "gerrymander"
Norway's independence proclaimed
University College London founded; known until 1836 as London University
American Physiological Society organizes in Boston
Gaetano Donizetti's opéra comique "La Fille du Regiment" (The Daughter of the Regiment) premieres at the Salle de la Bourse in Paris, France
Inaugural first-class cricket match in Australia; Tasmania vs. Victoria in Launceston; Tasmania wins by 3 wickets
First British public female toilet opens (Bedford Street, London)
For the 1st time coal gas is used to light major streets in San Francisco
Kassa Hailu is crowned Tewodros II, Emperor of Ethiopia, by Abuna Salama III in a ceremony at the church of Derasge Maryam
First vision of the Virgin Mary to 14-year-old Bernadette of Lourdes, France
US House unanimously passes resolution guaranteeing noninterference with slavery in any state
Spanish Cortes fires king Amadeus I
First US bicycle club, the Boston Bicycle Club, forms
First weekly weather report is published in the UK
Japan adopts the Meiji Constitution, the first Diet or Parliament convenes in 1890
-17°F (-27.2°C) in Braemar, Grampian (UK record)
Georgetown became part of Washington, D.C.
White Rose Mission opens on East 97th Street, NYC
Owen Smith of NC, AME Zion minister, named US minister to Liberia
-15°F (-26°C), Washington, D.C. (district record)
Police beat up universal suffrage demonstrators in Brussels
James Blackstone, Seattle, bowls 299½-last pin breaks but stands
Pope Pius X publishes encyclical Vehementer nos
De Master's Dutch government resigns
Passenger ship Larchmont sinks by Block Island, off Rhode Island, 322 die
Heemskerk's government begins in Holland
Baltimore Symphony Orchestra presents its 1st concert, led by Gustav Strube of the Peabody Institute, at the Lyric Theatre
Germany and Austria-Hungary notify the US that they will sink any armed merchant ships starting on 1 March
Ambassador Theater opens at 215 W 49th St NYC
Influential arts festival Semana de Arte Moderna (Modern Art Week) opens in São Paulo - the beginning of Modernism in Brazil
US intervention army leaves Honduras
UK transfers administration of Tokelau (Union) Islands in South Pacific to New Zealand
II Winter Olympic Games open in St Moritz, Switzerland
Vatican City, the world's smallest country, becomes an enclave of Rome
73°F highest temperature ever recorded in Cleveland in February
Johan Grøttumsbraaten leads a Norwegian medal sweep of the Nordic combined event at the Lake Placid Games; retains combined title after also winning in St. Moritz
-11°F (-24°C), Ifrane, Morocco (African record low)
First US airplane flight with an automobile slung beneath the fuselage occurs in New York
Pumping begins to build Treasure Island in San Francisco Bay
44-day sit-down strike at General Motors in Flint Mich ends
Sicherheitsdienst complains about Dutch anti German sentiments
"Archie" comic book debuts
Transport #47 departs with French Jews to nazi-Germany
German troops reconquer Aprilia, Italy
U-424 sunk off Ireland
First gas turbine propeller-driven airplane flight test in Downey, California
World War II: Royal Navy's Operation Deadlight ends after scuttling 116 of 156 captured German U-boats
Billy Griffith scores cricket century on debut Eng v WI, out for 140
John Costello follows Eamon Da Valera as premier of Ireland
Test cricket debut of Frank Worrell for the West Indies vs. England at Port-of-Spain
Willie Pep recaptures world featherweight boxing title
"Rag Mop" by The Ames Brothers hits #1
4th British Empire Games close in Auckland, New Zealand
Englishman Jack Holden wins Empire Games marathon in Auckland, New Zealand in 2:32:57; runs last 9 miles barefoot after his shoes fell apart during the race
PresidentEisenhowerrefuses clemency appeal for Rosenberg couple
USSR breaks diplomatic relations with Israel
6th Emmy Awards: I Love Lucy, Donald O'Connor & Eve Arden win
British diplomats Guy Burgess and Donald Maclean deny working as spies for Soviet Russia after reappearing in the Soviet Union after going missing 5 years earlier
KUMV TV channel 8 in Williston, ND (NBC) begins broadcasting
BBC pioneering series "Your Life in Their Hands" first broadcast presented by Dr. Charles Fletcher, 1st major TV series to deal with medicine
Marshal Chen Yi succeeds Chu En-lai as Chinese Minister of Foreign Affairs
Ruth Carol Taylor is 1st African-American woman hired as flight attendant, Ithaca NY
WTVC TV channel 9 in Chattanooga, TN (ABC) begins broadcasting
Vinoo Mankad ends his final Test Cricket (v WI at Delhi)
Robert Weaver sworn in as Administrator of the Housing and Home Finance Agency, the 1st African American to be appointed to a US cabinet-level position
American chef Julia Child's show "The French Chef" premieres on WGBH in Boston - one of the first cooking shows in the US
CIA Domestic Operations Division created
The Beatles record 10 of the 14 tracks for their first UK album "Please Please Me" in marathon recording session
Greeks and Turks begin fighting in Limassol, Cyprus
Taiwan drops diplomatic relations with France
The Beatles first concert appearance in US, at the Washington Coliseum in Washington, D.C.
Braves propose to pay 5 cents from each ticket to bring a new team to Milwaukee
Israeli-Jordan border fight
Jeffrey Kramer survives 76-m jump, Washington Bridge, Hudson River New York
Madison Square Garden III closes Madison Square Garden IV opens (NYC)
Dorey Funk Jr beats Gene Kiniski in Tampa, to become NWA champ
26.37 cm (10.38") of rainfall, Mt Washington, NH (state 24-hr rec)
Japan becomes the fourth nation to put a satellite (Ohsumi) in orbit
Montreal Canadien John Believau scores his 500th NHL goal
US, UK, USSR, and others sign the Seabed Arms Control Treaty outlawing nuclear weapons on the ocean floor
First one-day international for Pakistan and New Zealand
Philadelphia 76ers lose their 20th consecutive NBA game
First Major League Baseball arbitration case; Minnesota Twins pitcher Dick Woodson seeking $29,000 wins; Twins offered $23,000
Libya nationalizes three US oil companies that had not agreed to 51 percent nationalization in September
Titan-Centaur rocket test launch fails
TV drama “Sarah T: Portrait of A Teenage Alcoholic”, starringLinda Blair, premieres on US network NBC
Clifford Alexander Jr confirmed as 1st African American US Secretary of Army
John Curry wins Britain's first Olympic gold medal in figure skating at Innsbruck Winter Games; finishes men's singles ahead of Soviet skater Vladimir Kovalyov and Canadian Toller Cranston
20.2-kg lobster caught off Nova Scotia (heaviest known crustacean)
16 Unification church couples wed in NYC
EOKA disbands in Cyprus
Australia all out 83 v India at MCG chasing 143 to win
4th largest snowfall in NYC history (18"(46 cm))
10th NASA Space Shuttle Mission (41B): Challenger 4 returns to Earth
Kent Hrbek signs 5-year $6 million contract with Minnesota Twins
King Hussein of Jordan & PLO leader Arafat sign accord
Activist Anatoly Scharansky released by USSR, leaves country
Iran begins Fajr-8 offensive against Iraq
British Airways begins trading stocks
Philippines constitution goes into effect
US performs nuclear test at Nevada Test Site
US Episcopal Church Diocese of Massachusetts installs Barbara Harris (59) as 1st female bishop of a US Episcopal church
Unrepresented Nations and Peoples Organization (UNPO) forms in The Hague, Netherlands
F-16 jet crashes at residential district of Hengelo, Netherlands (No deaths)
Michael Johnson runs indoor world record 400m (44.97 sec)
Irina Privalova runs world record 60m indoor (6.92 secs)
Space Shuttle STS-60 (Discovery 18) lands
Danyon Loader swims world record 400m freestyle (340.46)
Sandra Volker swims European record 50m backstroke (27.67 sec)
Space shuttle STS-63 (Discovery 19), lands
West Indies score 5-660 against New Zealand in Test cricket
STS 82 (Discovery 22) launches
Lyrics to "Candle in the Wind 1997" auctioned for $442,500
Pluto moves further away from the sun than Neptune regaining its status as solar system's outermost planet, a title it will retain for 228 years
A national referendum in Portugal legalises non-therapeutic abortion when requested by the woman during the first 10 weeks of pregnancy.
In East Timor, assassination attempts made on independence activists Xanana Gusmao and Jose Ramos-Horta. Both fail.
Israeli Air Force conducts four air strikes in Gaza Strip
18 coal miners are killed in an explosion in the Komi region, Russia
11 people are killed after a grenade was tossed into a movie theatre in Peshawar, Pakistan
77 people are killed after a military transport plane crashes in Algeria
Peace talks resume in Geneva as leaders attempt to bring an end to the civil war in Syria
Slopestyle freestyle skiing event makes Olympic debut at Sochi Winter Games; Canadian Dara Howell is inaugural women's gold medallist
Francesco Schettino, Captain of the Costa Concordia that ran aground 2012, is convicted of manslaughter in Grosseto and sentenced to 16 years in jail
Discover of gravitational waves (through collision of two black holes) announced by physicists from the Laser Interferometer Gravitational-Wave Observatory (Ligo)
Last of Oregon militia occupying Malheur wildlife refuge surrenders to authorities after 41 days
Riot between rival drug cartels at a prison in Monterrey, Mexico, leaves 52 dead
Sixth Democratic presidential candidates debate, broadcast on CNN and PBS, and held in Milwaukee, Wisconsin
Dutchman Sven Kramer becomes only male speed skater to win same Olympic event 3 times, claiming gold in the 5,000m at Pyongchang; first man to win total 8 Olympic medals in the sport
Norway sweeps the medals in the Olympic 30k cross country skiathlon at Pyeongchang; Simen Hegstad Krüger wins gold ahead of teammates Martin Johnsrud Sundby & Hans Christer Holund
Russian airliner crashes south-east of Moscow, killing all 71 on board
Artificial Intelligence system meant to assist diagnosis in the future, pitted against physicians in test to diagnose 600,000 patients in results published in "Nature Medicine", A.I. won, just
"The Hot Wing King" a play by Katori Hall premieres off-Broadway at Signature Theatre in New York (Pulitzer Prize 2021 for Drama)
Largest wave ever surfed by a woman by Brazilian Maya Gabeira riding a 73.5ft (22.4m) wave at Nazaré, Portugal
Snow falls in Baghdad, Iraq, for only the second time in a century
US Attorney General, William Barr recommends cutting suggested sentence of Roger Stone, prompting prosecutors to resign
California's COVID-19 death toll overtakes New York's, reaching 45,496 amid first sign of falling infection numbers (Johns Hopkins)
UK Kent COVID-19 strain set "to sweep the world" and become the dominant strain globally according to UK scientist Sharon Peacock after strain detected in over 50 countries (later named Alpha)
World's second oldest person, French nun Sister André, celebrates her 117th birthday after surviving COVID-19 in Toulon
Australia lists the koala as endangered for the first time in Queensland, New South Wales and ACT after a steep decline in numbers
Africa Cup of Nations (AFCON) final; hosts Ivory Coast defeat Nigeria 2-1 at Alassane Ouattara Stadium, Abidjan
Argentina's first female saint María Antonia de Paz y Figueroa, (Mama Antula) canonised at a ceremony attended by President Javier Milei at the Vatican
Former Finnish Prime Minister Alexander Stubb wins the presidential election over the Green Party
Super Bowl LVIII is the most-watched TV program in US history, averaging 123.4 million viewers across television and streaming platforms
Tiberius Claudius Caesar Britannicus, heir to the Roman Emperorship, dies under mysterious circumstances in Rome, clearing the way forNeroto become Emperor
Holy Roman EmperorCharles Vand English KingHenry VIIIsign anti-French covenant
Samuel Adams' letter opposing taxes in the Townshend Act circulates around the American colonies
William Grenvillebecomes Prime Minister of the United Kingdom following the death of William Pitt the Younger
American inventorRobert Fultonpatents the steamboat
Giuseppe Verdi's dramatic opera "I Lombardi" premieres at the Teatro alla Scala in Milan, Italy
US President-electAbraham Lincolntakes train from Spingfield IL to Washington, D.C.
Oscar Wilde's "Salome" premieres in Paris
Anton Bruckner's 9th Symphony premieres by the Wiener Concertvereinsorchester, under conductor Ferdinand Löwe, in his own unauthorized arrangement, at Wiener Musikvereinssaal in Vienna, Austria

US Congress adopts the Expedition Act, which authorizes the Attorney General to 'expedite' anti-trust cases through the courts, reflecting growing popular support for PresidentTheodore Roosevelt's "trust busting" campaign
Emma Goldmanarrested for lecturing on birth control

US PresidentWoodrow Wilsonmakes another speech before Congress and announces 'the Four Principles' - freedom of navigation, and end to secret diplomacy, and similar items - that supplement his Fourteen Points
Friedrich Ebert(SPD) elected president of Germany
"April Showers" byAl Jolsonhits #1
Eugene O'Neill's play "Dynamo" premieres in NYC
Steve Casey beatsLou Theszin Boston to win the American Wrestling Association World Heavyweight Championship
World's first science fiction TV program is a broadcast of the play R.U.R. byKarel Čapek
German GeneralErwin Rommelarrives in Tripoli
US GeneralEisenhowerselected to command the allied armies in Europe; British General Montgomery not best pleased
"Lovesick Blues" single is released byHank Williams(Cashbox "Best Hillbilly Record of the Year," Billboard Song of the Year 1949)
Kwame Nkrumahand his Convention People's Party wins 1st legislative general election in the Gold Coast (Ghana) in a landslide
NHL Players Association forms (NYC), Red Wings'Ted Lindsayelected president
China's 1st National People's Congress adopts the first edition ofHanyu Pinyincreated byZhou Youguangas the official romanization system for Standard Mandarin Chinese
Jack Paarwalks off his TV show
San Francisco Giants outfielderWillie Mayssigns highest contract, $130,000 per year
John Lennonpays £1,344 fines for 96 protesting South African rugby team playing in Scotland
First sub-17-minute 1,500m female freestyle swim is achieved by Australian swimmerShane Gouldin 16m 56.9s
US Secretary of StateHenry Kissingerunveils Nixon Administration's seven-point "Project Independence" plan to make the U.S. energy independent
Margaret ThatcherdefeatsEdward Heathfor leadership of the British Conservative Party
25th hat trick in NY Islander history,Denis Potvin's 2nd
China lifts a ban on works ofAristotle,William ShakespeareandCharles Dickens
43 million watch "Elvis!" on ABC
Iran's Prime Minister Shapour Bakhtiar resigns after losing support of the military;Ayatollah Khomeiniseizes power
Polish premier Jozef Pinkowski replaced byWojciech Jaruzelski
Ozzie Smithfor Garry Templeton trade between San Diego Padres and St. Louis Cardinals finally goes through, after 62 days
"Weird Al" Yankovic records "Ricky" and "Buckingham Blues" to complete his debut LP, at Scotti Brothers Studio in Santa Monica, California
Single "Total Eclipse of the Heart" sung by Bonnie Tyler and composed byJim Steinmanis released
Wayne Gretzkysets NHL short handed season scoring record at 11
In a huge upset, James "Buster" Douglas KOsMike Tysonin 10th round in Tokyo, Japan to win the world heavyweight boxing title
Nelson Mandelais released after 27 years of imprisonment in South Africa
President Clintonselects Janet Reno to be first female US Attorney General
Bill Parcellsbecomes head coach of NY Jets
KVBC-FM (Las Vegas) offersMonica Lewinsky$5m for an exclusive tell-all, she ignores the request
US Vice PresidentDick Cheneyaccidentally shoots and injures Harry Whittington while on a quail hunt on a ranch in Riviera, Texas
49th Grammy Awards: [Dixie] Chicks -"Not Ready To Make Nice";Carrie Underwood(new artist and female country vocal);Bob Dylan;Mary J. Blige; Red Hot Chili Peppers win
Uri Gellerpurchases Lamb Island, Scotland, previously known for its witch trials
Egyptian Revolution culminates in the resignation ofHosni Mubarakand the transfer of power to the Supreme Military Council after 18 days of protests during the Arab Spring
PopeBenedict XVIannounces his resignation effective February 28, becoming the first pope to resign since 1415
MOMA opens a major solo exhibition for Brazilian artistTarsila do Amaralin New York
Bernie Sanderswins the New Hampshire Democratic primary
Caitlin Clarkscores a season-high 39 points for Iowa against Nebraska, breaking the single-game scoring record for Pinnacle Bank Arena
US PresidentJoe Bidenrescinds the national emergency order used byDonald Trumpto fund the border wall with Mexico
Super Bowl LVIII, Allegiant Stadium, Las Vegas, Nevada: Kansas City Chiefs win back to back titles and 3rd in 5 years with an overtime 25-22 win over the SF 49ers; MVP:Patrick Mahomes, KC, QB
Usherperforms the halftime show at Super Bowl LVIII with guestsAlicia Keys, will.i.am, Lil Jon, H.E.R. and Ludacris

Declaration of Liberated Europe is signed at the Yalta Conference byFranklin D. Roosevelt, Winston Churchill, and Joseph Stalin, declaring that liberated nations are to establish democratic governments through free elections
Ladislas, King of Naples (1386-1414), born in Naples (d. 1414)
Poggio Bracciolini, Italian humanist who rediscovered lost classical texts, born in Terranuova, Republic of Florence (d. 1459)
Elizabeth of York, Queen Consort of England and wife of King Henry VII, born in Westminster Palace, London (d. 1503)

Honoré d'Urfé, French writer (L'Astrée), born in Marseille, France (d. 1625)

Rachilde [Marguerite Vallette-Eymery], French author (Monsieur Venus), born near Périgueux, France (d. 1953)

Farouk I, last King of Egypt (1936-52), born in Abdeen Palace, Cairo, Egypt (d. 1965)

Michel Sénéchal, French operatic tenor, and educator, born in Paris (d. 2018)
